items.prompt: identifies defining attributes of common polygons including side count, equal sides, and right angles where appropriate
items.expected_evidence: name and sort triangles, quadrilaterals, pentagons, hexagons, and other familiar polygons by visible attributes
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner completes the task accurately and justifies the choice.

items.prompt: sorts and classifies polygons into named categories using stated attribute rules
items.expected_evidence: distinguish defining attributes from non-defining features such as orientation or size
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner explains the reasoning using correct geometric language or supported demonstration.

items.prompt: Diagnose a peer error aligned to hierarchy_reversal.
items.expected_evidence: justify category membership using explicit geometric language
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner identifies the mistaken rule and states the correct reasoning.

items.prompt: succeeds across drawings, sort tasks, and compare/classify prompts
items.expected_evidence: reason about subclass relationships inside polygon families
items.scoring_guidance: Meets when the learner succeeds in a less-familiar context without loss of concept integrity.
